content:
# Hygiene Scoreboard Template

**Tool ID:** T012-03
**Block:** B012 (Hygiene Culture System)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Dashboard
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes weekly review
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

Your command view. Everything you need to see in one place -- trends, gaps, and wins -- so you can lead with clarity instead of gut feeling. This scoreboard makes hygiene performance visible to the entire team, creating positive peer accountability and celebrating consistency.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Visual display board for the staff area showing daily cleaning completion rates, weekly audit scores, and hygiene champion recognition. Designed to motivate through transparency rather than pressure.

This tool supports **Hygiene Culture System** (B012) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (updates weekly); Shift Manager (updates daily)
**Time Required:** 2 minutes daily to update; 15 minutes weekly to review and reset
**Frequency:** Updated daily; reviewed and reset weekly (Monday)

**What You'll Need:**
- This scoreboard printed on A3, laminated, and posted in the staff area
- Whiteboard marker (for writing on laminated surface) or printed weekly sheets
- Data from Daily Cleaning Checklist (T011-01), Hygiene Huddle records (T012-02), and Weekly Cleaning Audit (T011-04)

**Steps:**
1. Print or prepare the scoreboard at the start of each week
2. Each day, the Shift Manager updates the daily metrics after reviewing the cleaning checklist
3. Each week, the Manager reviews the full scoreboard and completes the weekly summary
4. Recognise the hygiene champion at the weekly team briefing
5. Reset for the new week every Monday

---

## Dashboard Overview

### This Week: ___/___/______ to ___/___/______

### Key Metrics

| Metric | Target | Mon | Tue | Wed | Thu | Fri | Sat | Sun | Week Avg | RAG |
|--------|--------|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|----------|-----|
| Daily cleaning checklist completion | 100% | _% | _% | _% | _% | _% | _% | _% | _____% | GREEN: 95%+ / AMBER: 85-94% / RED: <85% |
| Hygiene huddle completed | Yes | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ___/7 | GREEN: 7/7 / AMBER: 5-6 / RED: <5 |
| Handwashing log entries | 8+ per shift | __ | __ | __ | __ | __ | __ | __ | _____ | GREEN: 8+ avg / AMBER: 5-7 / RED: <5 |
| Customer toilet checks | 3x daily | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ___/7 | GREEN: daily / AMBER: most days / RED: missed 3+ |
| No cleaning complaints from customers | Zero | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | _____ | GREEN: 0 / AMBER: 1 / RED: 2+ |

### Weekly Cleaning Audit Score (from T011-04)

| Area | Score (out of 10) | Last Week | Trend | Notes |
|------|------------------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Bar and espresso station | ___/10 | ___/10 | ↑ ↓ → | |
| Kitchen and prep area | ___/10 | ___/10 | ↑ ↓ → | |
| Customer seating area | ___/10 | ___/10 | ↑ ↓ → | |
| Customer toilet | ___/10 | ___/10 | ↑ ↓ → | |
| Storage and back of house | ___/10 | ___/10 | ↑ ↓ → | |
| **Overall** | **___/50** | **___/50** | ↑ ↓ → | Target: 40+/50 |

### Period Summary

| Period | Highlights | Concerns | Actions |
|--------|-----------|----------|---------|
| This week | | | |
| Last week | | | |
| This month | | | |

### Hygiene Champion of the Week

| Week | Champion | Why They Were Recognised |
|------|---------|------------------------|
| This week | _____ | *e.g., "Consistently completed all closing cleaning tasks to a high standard and restocked handwash basins without being asked"* |
| Last week | _____ | |
| 2 weeks ago | _____ | |
| 3 weeks ago | _____ | |

**Recognition criteria:**
- Consistently completes cleaning tasks without reminders
- Proactively identifies and fixes hygiene issues
- Supports teammates with cleaning during busy periods
- Demonstrates proper technique (handwashing, surface sanitising)
- Spotted something important during the hygiene huddle that prevented an issue

### Compliance and Completion

| Area | Target % | Actual % | Gap | Owner |
|------|---------|---------|-----|-------|
| Opening cleaning tasks completed | 100% | _____% | _____% | Opening Shift Lead |
| Closing cleaning tasks completed | 100% | _____% | _____% | Closing Shift Lead |
| Espresso machine daily backflush | 100% | _____% | _____% | Head Barista |
| Weekly deep clean completed | 100% | _____% | _____% | Kitchen Lead |

### Action Items from This Review

| # | Action | Owner | Due Date | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-------|----------|----------|--------|
| 1 | | | | High / Medium / Low | Open / Done |
| 2 | | | | High / Medium / Low | Open / Done |
| 3 | | | | High / Medium / Low | Open / Done |

**Weekly Decision:** Overall hygiene status: GREEN On Track / AMBER Needs Attention / RED Intervention Required

**Manager sign-off:** _______________  Date: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Scoreboard Becomes Stale Because Nobody Updates It

**Why it happens:** The first two weeks are enthusiastic. By week three, the Shift Manager forgets to update the daily metrics. The scoreboard shows data from two weeks ago and nobody looks at it.
**How to prevent:** Make the daily update part of the shift handover. The outgoing Shift Manager updates the scoreboard BEFORE handing over. It takes 2 minutes. If the scoreboard is not updated, the handover is not complete.
**If it is already happening:** Reset the board this Monday. Announce: "We are restarting the scoreboard. I will update it daily for the first week to get the rhythm back."

### Risk 2: Team Sees the Scoreboard as Competition Rather Than Collaboration

**Why it happens:** Naming a "champion" each week can create rivalry or resentment if it always goes to the same person or if other team members feel overlooked.
**How to prevent:** Rotate recognition broadly. Recognise different types of contribution each week -- not always the fastest cleaner, but also the person who spotted an issue, restocked supplies, or helped a colleague. Make it a celebration, not a competition.
**If it is already happening:** Broaden the criteria. Consider team achievements: "This week the entire team hit 100% cleaning completion for the first time in a month." Collective recognition builds culture better than individual awards.

### Risk 3: Scores Are Inflated to Avoid Difficult Conversations

**Why it happens:** The Shift Manager gives 9/10 for the kitchen audit because they do not want to upset the Kitchen Lead. The real score should be 6/10 but nobody addresses the gap. The scoreboard shows green when reality is amber.
**How to prevent:** Use specific, observable criteria for each score. A 10/10 kitchen means: all surfaces sanitised, all boards in rack, floor mopped, bins empty, handwash basin stocked, fridge organised. If any of these are not done, the score cannot be 10. Document what was missing so the score is defensible.
**If it is already happening:** Calibrate the scoring by doing one audit together (Manager and Shift Manager). Agree what each score level looks like in practice. Take photos of a "10/10 kitchen" and a "7/10 kitchen" to make the standard visual and objective.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** A laminated A3 sheet on the staff notice board is sufficient. Use a whiteboard marker for daily updates and wipe clean each week.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand may have a digital hygiene tracking system. Use this physical scoreboard as a team-facing complement to the digital system. Staff respond to what they can see in their break room.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify to 3 key metrics: cleaning completion, handwashing, and one rotating focus. Post on a mini board inside the kiosk.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add a metric for "bakery clean-down completion" covering oven, proving racks, and flour dust control.

**Making it yours:**
- Adjust the metrics to match your actual cleaning checklist and audit criteria
- Set realistic targets -- 100% is the goal, but celebrate improvement from 80% to 90% too
- Change the hygiene champion criteria each month to keep it fresh
- Consider a monthly "hygiene team score" with a small reward for hitting targets (e.g., coffee on the house, team lunch)

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T012-01 -- Hygiene Culture Charter (L2, template)
- T012-02 -- Daily Hygiene Huddle Protocol (L1, guide)
- T012-04 -- Peer Observation Feedback Form (L2, template)
- T012-05 -- Quarterly Hygiene Culture Survey (L3, assessment)

**Related blocks:**
- B009
- B010
- B011

**Parent block:** B012 -- Hygiene Culture System
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
